title: is_glob bool param should be enum
description: cache_patterns_in_clause(clause, is_glob: bool, cache) in lib.rs uses a bool to distinguish between two pattern kinds: glob vs regex. This is a two-variant enum in disguise. Idiomatic Rust would define enum PatternKind { Glob, Regex } and pass that instead. The bool makes call sites like cache_patterns_in_clause(&clause, false, cache) and cache_patterns_in_clause(&clause, true, cache) opaque — a reader must look up the signature to know which bool means which kind. An enum makes the intent clear at the call site.
